ogTitle: Hurlstone Park vs Hurstville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hurlstone Park and Hurstville.
heading: Hurlstone Park vs Hurstville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hurlstone Park, New South Wales and Hurstville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hurlstone Park has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Hurstville (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hurlstone Park or Hurstville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hurlstone Park has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Hurstville scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hurlstone Park scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Hurstville scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hurlstone Park or Hurstville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hurlstone Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
